# Subcommand aliases must behave identically to their canonical name.
# rm/delete is covered in rm_lifecycle.txt; show/get in show_get.txt;
# this file pins the remaining pair: add → create, new and ls → list.

# `eon create` / `eon new` are the same code path as `eon add`.
exec eon create --cron '@hourly' --name viacreate -- echo a
stdout 'added job [0-9A-Za-z]+'
exec eon new --cron '@hourly' --name vianew -- echo b
stdout 'added job [0-9A-Za-z]+'

# `eon list` is the alias for `eon ls`. JSON shape must match.
exec eon list --json
stdout '"name": "viacreate"'
stdout '"name": "vianew"'

# Alias help still surfaces the same parent text.
exec eon create --help
stdout 'CRON SYNTAX'
exec eon new --help
stdout 'ONE-SHOT TIME'
exec eon list --help
stdout '--kind'
stdout '--status'
